Description: Recruits signaling proteins (like PKC) to membranes via interaction with DAG/phorbol esters.
Calcium-dependent or independent membrane targeting module involved in signal transduction and trafficking.
Acts as a ubiquitous intracellular membrane tether recruiting proteins to diverse compartment membranes.
Zinc-finger-like domain essential for highly selective recruitment to early endosomes.
Structural lipid-binding domain found in sorting nexins and phospholipases involved in vesicular trafficking.
Found in endocytic proteins; inserts an alpha-helix to drive membrane curvature and recruit clathrin machinery.
Universal adaptor domain for clathrin coat assembly; structurally similar to ENTH but utilizes basic residues instead of a wedging helix.
Conserved beta-propeller structures acting as distinct lipid sensors during autophagy and autophagosome biogenesis.
Functions in membrane-tethered transcription factors; links G-protein signaling to transcription.
Homologous to PH domains; found in myotubularins and required for intracellular membrane targeting.
Conserved structural module functioning as a co-chaperone that binds and stimulates Hsp70 heat-shock proteins.
Banana-shaped dimeric domain that acts as a physical scaffold to sense or induce tubular membrane curvature.
BAR domains equipped with an N-terminal Amphipathic Helix that wedges into the bilayer to drive extreme curvature.
Amphipathic alpha-helix lacking basic residues; inserts directly into membrane packing defects caused by curvature.
Primary Target(s): Lipid packing defects; convex membranes (ER/Golgi vesicles)

Description: Regulates kinase localization by targeting acidic membrane patches (often substituted for typo 'Kaon').
Unstructured sequences serving as electrostatically driven membrane anchors.
Unstructured sequences serving as electrostatically driven membrane anchors.
Plasmodium helical interspersed subtelomeric domain involved in exporting parasite proteins across host membranes (historically referred to as the PRESAN domain).
Primary Target(s): Host cell membrane components; structural modifications

Description: Conserved N-terminal sequence motif that guides translocator/effector entry into host plant cells.
Primary Target(s): Host plasma membrane lipids; translocation machinery

Description: Conserved structural fold that is frequently arranged in tandem repeats downstream of the RXLR motif to modulate target affinity.
Mature-parasite-infected Erythrocyte Surface Antigen erythrocyte cytoskeleton-binding motif
ToxA conserved RGD host cell import motif
Motif Notes: Flanking 10 aa, totalling 23aa, from 25 Haplotypes as of 2026
Primary Target(s): RGD residues required for host cell translocation

Description: RIPP biosynthesis.
Motif Notes: Twin HXXHC sequences found within the active sites of oxidative maturation enzymes (specifically DUF3328 domain proteins). These enzymes catalyze key chemical modifications, such as side-chain macrocyclization.

Description: Shuttles lipids across aqueous cytosol between distinct organelle membrane contact sites.
Specialized lipid transfer modules; CERT shuttles ceramide while GLTP manages glycosphingolipids.
Oxysterol-binding protein-related domains acting as critical sensors and transporters at contact sites.
Hydrophobic pocket domain binding small lipophilic molecules; found in Sec14p and visual cycle proteins.
Conserved sequence string discovered and mapped via MOPRO computational frameworks to define explicit structural affinity configurations with neutral or acidic sphingolipid variants.
Responsible for folding and inserting outer membrane proteins (OMPs) into Gram-negative bacterial envelopes.
Chaperone-like domain linked to the BAM complex that guides unfolded outer membrane proteins into the translocator.
Lipid-binding / Bactericidal permeability-increasing proteins; binds bacterial envelopes to neutralize endotoxins.
Plasmodium export element sequence motif that targets and directs intracellular parasite effector proteins across the parasitophorous vacuole membrane into the host erythrocyte cytosol.
Primary Target(s): Endoplasmic reticulum lumen processing machinery (Plasmepsin V); host erythrocyte trafficking components

Description: Canonical primary N-terminal dibasic site targeting by the Golgi-localized proprotein convertase Kex2 to drive prodomain removal or effector activation.
Primary Target(s): Proprotein convertases; mature secretome processing loops

Description: Canonical secondary dibasic processing sequence targeted during proprotein convertase cleavage cycles within the fungal secretory pipeline.
Primary Target(s): Proprotein convertases; mature secretome processing loops

Description: Alternative dibasic recognition profile motif targeted during processing by Golgi convertase systems.
Primary Target(s): Proprotein convertases; mature secretome processing loops

Description: Alternative basic recognition motif targeted during endoproteolytic maturation events in the Golgi apparatus.
Primary Target(s): Proprotein convertases; mature secretome processing loops

Description: Extended aliphatic-dependent endoproteolytic consensus profile sequence where position 1 contains leucine or an equivalent branched aliphatic amino acid, position 4 contains arginine, and position 3 displays an amino acid preference ordering of K > R > T > P > L > I.
Primary Target(s): Proprotein convertases; mature secretome processing loops

Description: Cytoskeletal organizer that anchors transmembrane proteins or receptors to the underlying actin network.
Central scaffolding domain that organizes signaling receptor complexes at the plasma membrane.
Recruited directly to phosphorylated receptors to propagate signaling cascades.
Works in tandem with SH2 to mediate assembly of specific multiprotein signaling complexes.
Catalytic and regulatory kinase modules; PKC specifically localizes using tandem C1/C2 modules.
Primary Target(s): Target protein phosphorylation sites; cAMP (PKA); DAG (PKC)

Description: Calcium-dependent carbohydrate-recognition domain involved in immunity and pathogen cell walls.

7-transmembrane receptor domain characteristic of the rhodopsin-like G-protein coupled receptor superfamily; transduces extracellular signals into intracellular responses via heterotrimeric G-proteins.
Primary Target(s): Heterotrimeric G-proteins; extracellular ligands (hormones, neurotransmitters, odorants, photons)

Description: A highly stable protein structural motif containing an embedded framework of three interconnected disulfide bonds that form a closed molecular knot topology; found across growth factors and diverse plant/animal toxins.
Primary Target(s): Cell-surface signaling receptors; extracellular matrix components

Description: A ubiquitous carbohydrate-binding module found in extracellular proteins and effectors that recognizes and binds chitin or peptidoglycan polymers to modulate host-pathogen interactions or suppress host immunity.
Primary Target(s): Chitin fragments; N-acetylglucosamine polymers; peptidoglycan strands

Description: A small extracellular domain characterized by six conserved cysteines that form disulfide bridges. It is primarily found in insect peritrophic matrix proteins, animal chitinases, and baculoviruses.
Originally discovered in the rubber tree (Hevea brasiliensis), this domain features a conserved four-disulfide core. It is found in plant lectins, defense proteins, and fungal chitinases.
A conserved modular domain found in a variety of fungal, bacterial, and plant proteins. In enzymes like chitinases, it helps anchor the protein to the chitin polymer before cleavage.
Often found in lytic polysaccharide monooxygenases (LPMOs), this domain binds to crystalline polysaccharides (including chitin and cellulose) to assist in enzymatic oxidative cleavage.
Found in fungal cell wall integrity and stress-response proteins, it acts as a carbohydrate-binding module capable of recognizing various glycans.
Frequently found in tandem in bacterial and fungal enzymes (like chitinases) to target the active site directly to the insoluble chitin substrate.
